claim: JAX's SPMD (Single Program Multiple Data) paradigm makes distributed training feel like programming for a single device while handling multi-device orchestration transparently
actionable: When scaling JAX/Flax NNX models across devices, write code as if targeting one device; JAX's SPMD semantics and sharding specs handle device distribution automatically
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Robert Crowe: JAX Made Simple

claim: Flax NNX (released 2024) is the canonical neural network library for JAX, replacing the older Flax Linen with a more Pythonic, object-oriented approach using NNX module subclasses
actionable: For new JAX projects, use Flax NNX's nn.Module subclass pattern instead of Flax Linen's functional approach for more intuitive model definition
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Robert Crowe: JAX Made Simple

claim: JAX achieves near-ideal scaling on 50,000+ TPUs and 80%+ EMFU (effective model flops utilization) on GPUs through XLA compilation and close partnership with Nvidia on GPU support
actionable: When you need state-of-the-art training efficiency for billion-parameter models, JAX + Flax NNX provides proven scaling beyond what PyTorch typically achieves
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Robert Crowe: JAX Made Simple

claim: Mesh-based sharding in JAX (e.g., 4x2 mesh for 8 devices) allows one-line code changes to switch between data parallelism, model parallelism, and tensor parallelism without rewriting model code
actionable: Define a logical mesh grid once (e.g., data_mesh=4, model_mesh=2), then use partition specs to map tensors; changing the mesh dimensions automatically switches parallelism strategy
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Robert Crowe: JAX Made Simple

claim: Random number generator state must be managed explicitly in Flax NNX (passed as parameter) to ensure reproducible model initialization across runs, not relying on global state
actionable: In Flax NNX __init__, accept a PRNGKey parameter and use it for all random operations; never use global random state to ensure full reproducibility
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Robert Crowe: JAX Made Simple

claim: Roof line analysis shows you must balance arithmetic intensity (work / data moved) to keep accelerators compute-bound, not IO-bound; modern training requires explicit parallelism strategy tuning
actionable: Profile your model using JAX's XProfile and roof line analysis; adjust data/model parallelism strategy to maximize MFU (model flops utilization) by reducing data movement relative to compute
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Robert Crowe: JAX Made Simple

claim: XLA compiler (below JAX) automatically optimizes Python code to machine code with automatic distribution support, but explicit parallelism tuning beyond automatic mode is needed for production efficiency
actionable: Start with JAX's automatic distribution mode for rapid prototyping, then profile and hand-tune sharding specs when approaching production to extract the last performance gains
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Robert Crowe: JAX Made Simple

claim: Tensor parallelism (splitting individual layers across devices) requires high inter-device bandwidth; it's a third-tier approach after data and model parallelism when memory constraints are extreme
actionable: Only use tensor parallelism (JAX sharding individual layers across devices) when a single shard still doesn't fit in device memory; validate inter-device bandwidth first
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Robert Crowe: JAX Made Simple
